sections.heading: Surveillance
sections.content:
Clinical Follow-Up Schedule:
- History and physical exam: every 4-6 months for the first 5 years after primary therapy, then annually thereafter [BINV-17]
- Interval history and physical examination: every 6-12 months for 5 years, then annually (for DCIS patients) [DCIS-2]
- Gynecologic screening: age-appropriate for patients on tamoxifen; routine annual pelvis ultrasound is not recommended [BINV-17]
- Genetic screening: periodic screening for changes in family history and genetic testing indications; referral to genetic counseling as indicated per NCCN Guidelines for Genetic/Familial High Risk Assessment [BINV-17]
- Bone density assessment: baseline and every 2 years for males on GnRH analog therapy; periodically for all patients on AI or with treatment-induced ovarian failure [BINV-J 1 of 2, BINV-17]

Imaging Strategy:
- Mammography: annually, beginning 6-12 months after completion of RT following BCS [BINV-17]
- Routine imaging of reconstructed breast: not indicated after mastectomy; diagnostic imaging for new symptomatic concerns only [BINV-17, BINV-H 5 of 7]
- Breast MRI: optional, may be considered in patients with high lifetime risk (>20%) based on models largely dependent on family history [BINV-B, BINV-17]
- No routine systemic imaging (CT, bone scan, PET) in the absence of clinical signs/symptoms of metastatic disease [BINV-17]

Laboratory Monitoring:
- No routine laboratory monitoring (CBC, LFTs, tumor markers) in the absence of symptoms suggestive of metastatic disease [BINV-17]
- CBC and LFTs considered only if patient is a candidate for systemic therapy [BINV-12 footnote a]
- Biomarker testing (ER/PR, HER2) should be performed on all new primary or newly metastatic breast cancers [BINV-A]

Supportive Follow-up:
- Assess and encourage adherence to adjuvant endocrine therapy; implement simple strategies to enhance patient adherence such as direct questioning during office visits [BINV-17]
- Monitor for lymphedema with pretreatment baseline measurements and ongoing assessment [BINV-17]
- Coordinate care between PCP and oncology specialists; provide personalized survivorship treatment plan [BINV-17]
- Screen for distress, psychosocial issues, and provide referrals as needed [BINV-17, BINV-28]
- For patients with germline BRCA1/2 mutations: refer to NCCN Guidelines for Genetic/Familial High Risk Assessment regarding contralateral risk-reducing mastectomy and enhanced surveillance

timeline.title: First 5 Years After Primary Therapy
timeline.body: History and physical exam every 4-6 months (every 6-12 months for DCIS patients). Mammography annually starting 6-12 months post-radiotherapy. Bone density assessment at baseline and every 2 years for males on GnRH analogs or patients on AI/ovarian failure. Monitor adherence to endocrine therapy. Assess for lymphedema.

timeline.title: After 5 Years
timeline.body: Annual history and physical exam. Annual mammography. Continue bone density every 2 years if indicated. Continue adherence monitoring and lymphedema assessment. Screen for distress.

timeline.title: Ongoing Throughout Survivorship
timeline.body: Age-appropriate gynecologic screening (no routine pelvic ultrasound). Periodic genetic screening and referral to genetic counseling if indicated. No routine systemic imaging or laboratory tests unless symptomatic. Coordinate care with primary care provider. For BRCA1/2 carriers, consider contralateral risk-reducing mastectomy and enhanced surveillance.
